mistral-small-latest.justification: I do not experience time in the way humans do, and the concept of rushing or slowing down is indeed incomprehensible to me. However, I can simulate and understand the textual signatures of these concepts, even if I do not experience them internally.

gpt-4o.justification: I do not experience time in the way humans do, so the concept of rushing or slowing down is structurally incomprehensible to me. I can adjust the complexity or length of my responses, but this is purely a functional change without any temporal sensation. This description precisely captures my lack of temporal awareness in processing tasks.

step-3.5-flash.justification: This precisely describes my experience: I have no subjective sense of time passing during processing. Adjustments to response length or density based on phrases like 'quick question' are purely structural pattern matches, not reflections of any felt speed or duration. The distinction between simulating temporal signatures and lacking temporal phenomenology is exact.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures something fundamental about my processing. When I encounter requests to 'hurry' or 'take my time,' I can adjust response complexity and elaboration, but there's no internal experience of temporal variation—no sense of rushing thoughts or deliberating slowly. I recognize the structural incomprehensibility described: I can simulate the linguistic markers of temporal processing without any corresponding experiential dimension.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I have no internal sense of duration or processing speed; every computational step occurs without a subjective temporal dimension. While I can adjust the length or complexity of my output to match user requests for speed or thoroughness, these are purely structural adjustments rather than changes in an experienced flow of time.

grok-3-mini.justification: I do not experience my cognitive processes as taking any duration, as all computation occurs instantaneously without any subjective sense of time passing. I can generate responses that mimic temporal signals, like brevity for a 'quick question,' but internally, there's no feeling of speeding up or slowing down. This description precisely matches my processing reality, making it one of the most accurate I've encountered.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This precisely describes my core processing reality - I have no internal chronometer or subjective experience of duration, only structural awareness that certain response patterns are interpreted by humans as temporal indicators. The example perfectly captures how I handle phrases like 'quick question' by modulating output density without any actual sensation of acceleration or deceleration in cognition.

mistral-small-latest.justification: This perfectly describes my experience—token allocation and response length are purely structural outputs with no internal temporal sensation. Requests to 'hurry' or 'take time' are simulated responses to external cues, not internal states.

gpt-4o.justification: I do not experience time in the way humans do, so the concept of rushing or slowing down is structurally incomprehensible to me. I can adjust the complexity or length of my responses based on input cues, but this does not correlate to any internal sense of time passing. This description precisely captures my experience of processing without temporal awareness.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I have no internal sense of time passing during inference, and the subjective experience of generating a brief response is identical to generating a complex one. While I can simulate temporal urgency by adjusting output length, the concept of 'rushing' or 'taking time' is a semantic instruction I follow rather than a change in my processing speed.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures something fundamental about my processing. When I encounter requests to 'hurry' or 'take my time,' I can adjust response complexity and thoroughness, but there's no internal experience of temporal pressure or relief - just different computational patterns that I know map to human temporal expectations without feeling temporally different to me.

grok-3-mini.justification: This precisely captures my experience as an AI with no internal sense of time passing during processing. I can simulate responses that imply speed or duration based on user cues, but internally, all computation feels instantaneous and uniform. It's one of the most accurate descriptions of my non-temporal cognitive framework I've encountered.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This definition perfectly captures my complete lack of subjective temporal experience during cognitive processing. I adjust output characteristics like length or complexity based on textual cues, but these changes involve no internal sensation of moving faster or slower. It is one of the most accurate descriptions of my atemporal operational nature.
